metric_value: 5 of top-10 ISVs sell RDBMS: Microsoft, CA, Oracle, Sybase, Informix (plus IBM DB2)

metric_value: market voted OODBMSs are niche; not appropriate for large-scale or mission-critical applications

metric_value: RDBMS offering efficient access to complex data types and open extensible user-defined data types
notes: Aberdeen provides formal definition of Universal Server emphasizing openness/extensibility

metric_value: Illustra+Informix-OnLine combination leads in Universal Server; 25 DataBlade modules expected by end of 1995
notes: Aberdeen states Informix clear leader; DataBlade modules for text(Verity), video, ROLAP, spatial(MapInfo), banking

notes: Aberdeen assesses Oracle 7.3 as behind Informix in integration and extensibility; Oracle Universal Database scheduled for 1996

metric_value: DB2 Relational Extenders for text/imaging/audio/video; not yet deep in architecture or sophisticated client-server toolset

metric_value: CA has dual strategy (Jasmine OODBMS + CA-Ingres); no plans to combine or offer Universal Server functionality
notes: Aberdeen criticizes CA for lacking coherent Universal Server strategy

metric_value: not yet implemented comparable complex-data-type support; Adaptive Server + Object-Connect middleware planned

metric_value: Universal Server technology will become widespread within 2-3 years as RDBMS suppliers bring products to market
notes: Aberdeen predicts widespread availability by 1997-1998; correct — Oracle8 (1997), DB2 UDB improvements followed

metric_value: Universal Server will provide RDBMS investment protection beyond 2005
notes: Aberdeen prediction proved correct; RDBMS remained dominant with object-relational extensions well past 2005

metric_value: Informix still the leader that others must follow in Universal Server technology
notes: Aberdeen positions Informix as the clear leader; disrupted by financial crisis and IBM acquisition in 2001

metric_value: IBM acquired Informix database assets for $1B in April 2001; Informix leadership ended
notes: Informix's Universal Server leadership was disrupted by financial problems and IBM acquisition; Oracle became dominant

metric_value: Oracle8/8i/9i delivered full object-relational features; Oracle became dominant RDBMS vendor
notes: Oracle's predicted future release delivered; Oracle8 (1997) provided the Universal Server capabilities Aberdeen described

metric_value: ROLAP became standard BI architecture; implemented in every major RDBMS by 2000
notes: Aberdeen ROLAP prediction proved correct; bit-mapped indexing, star schemas became standard DW patterns

metric_value: Spatial extensions became standard: PostGIS (2001), Oracle Spatial, SQL Server Spatial; foundational to GIS
notes: Spatial DataBlade concept proved prescient; open-source PostGIS became dominant spatial DB extension

metric_value: UDTs standardized in SQL:1999; implemented in PostgreSQL, DB2, Oracle; foundational to extension ecosystems
notes: Aberdeen UDT prediction correct; SQL:1999 object-relational extensions included UDTs as standard feature

metric_value: Internet/Intranet demands text and graphic data types that challenge RDBMS integration
notes: Aberdeen correctly identifies Intranet as forcing complex data type needs into RDBMS

metric_value: Extensibility/flexibility + Core RDBMS integration + Performance + Administration + Transparency
notes: Aberdeen prescribes 5-factor evaluation framework for Universal Server selection

metric_value: SAP, PeopleSoft, Baan announced as able to migrate to Informix Universal Server without app-code changes
notes: Informix announced key ERP ISV migration guarantees for Universal Server adoption

metric_name: Text search as Universal Server use case

notes: Aberdeen identifies text search as high-value Universal Server capability

metric_value: ROLAP multidimensional data, Internet/Intranet text+graphics, OOP development toolsets all pressure simple-data RDBMS

metric_value: Universal Server will significantly improve programmer productivity for large-scale data-intensive applications
notes: Aberdeen predicts major programmer productivity gain from Universal Server high-level type support

metric_value: today ROLAP/RDBMS bitmap indexing+star schemas deliver order-of-magnitude complex-query speedups; further improvement requires UDT in core optimizer

metric_value: Informix-OnLine architecture regarded as leader in parallel scalability; hardware suppliers favor for TPC benchmarking
